sessions.intent: Fix 6 failing PLang tests: 3 runtime bugs in MemoryStack.Get() and 3 incorrect .pr files

before.assumptions: The MemoryStack.Get() bug is on line 82 where +1 skip assumes dot separator. The 3 .pr file issues require builder rebuild.
before.risk: Low — single line runtime fix, well-tested. Builder rebuilds depend on LLM availability.

sessions.plan: Part 1: Fix MemoryStack.Get() array index navigation — change line 82 to check for dot before skipping. Add 4 C# tests. Part 2: Rebuild .pr files for Retry (missing onError), ListOps (off-by-one actions), SetMaxGzipSize (rewrite goal to settings syntax then rebuild).

actions.context: Fix line 82: check if char after rootName is dot before +1 skip. Array brackets should not be skipped.
Add 4 tests: arr[0].id, list[0].items[0].val, items[idx], items[1]
list.unique returned types.list wrapper (not IList), causing list.count to return 0. Changed to return raw List<object?> like list.split does.
Rebuilt Retry .pr files — now includes onError fields on throw steps
Rebuilt ListOps .pr — fixed off-by-one action alignment
Rewrote goal to use settings syntax, rebuilt .pr

after.notes: Remaining Condition test failure is pre-existing. list.range and list.flatten have same types.list wrapper issue but aren't tested yet.

sessions.intent: Test quality review of DataSource + Settings Bridge (coder v2 fixes for tester v2 findings)

before.assumptions: Tester v2 found 4 critical/major issues. Coder committed fixes in c4dbbb30 and e80c0dff. Need to verify fixes and hunt for remaining gaps.
before.risk: Test runner fix may not fully work. V1/v2 schema collision blocks PLang settings test.

sessions.plan: Run C# tests (1461), run PLang tests (23), analyze coverage, verify each v2 finding was addressed, check for new false greens, write test report.

actions.context: Reviewed all coder v2 changes addressing tester v2 findings F1-F4
Full code review of DataSource feature for test quality assessment

after.status: Verdict: APPROVED. After rebuilding PlangConsole with v1 table rename fix, all tests green.
after.health: C# tests: 1465/1465, PLang tests: 23/23 (both Settings tests pass)
after.notes: All v2 findings resolved. Remaining minor: test runner 2.8% coverage, SqliteDataSource error paths untested.

sessions.intent: Security audit of DataSource + Settings Bridge feature

before.assumptions: New SQLite persistence layer introduces SQL injection surface, JSON deserialization paths, and file I/O. Previous security audits (data-envelope-architecture, runtime2-settings) established the threat model and fixed all HIGH severity findings.
before.risk: Medium — new persistence layer with SQL and deserialization. SQLite is well-understood but the integration points need verification.

sessions.plan: Phase 1 (Blue Team): Map 8 attack surfaces — SQL injection, JSON deserialization, sync-over-async, actor→path mapping, error propagation, migration races, resource exhaustion, clone semantics. Phase 2 (Red Team): Attempt exploit construction for each surface.

actions.context: Full security review of all new and modified files in the DataSource feature

after.status: Verdict: PASS. No critical or high severity findings.
after.health: 1 medium finding (uncaught InvalidOperationException in DeserializeValue), 3 low findings (use-after-dispose, EnsureTable perf, migration race), 1 accepted-risk, 1 by-design.
after.notes: SQL injection properly mitigated. Path traversal impossible (actor names hardcoded). Error propagation in generated code correct. Medium finding requires specific conditions (externally-sourced deeply nested JSON stored in settings).
